questions.q: Which commander arrayed the Chakravyuha at Kurukshetra?
questions.explanation: Drona, then commanding the Kaurava army, arrayed the wheel formation.

questions.q: On which day of the Kurukshetra war was the Chakravyuha formed?
questions.explanation: Drona spun the wheel on the thirteenth day of the eighteen-day war.

questions.q: On what shape was the Chakravyuha modeled?
questions.explanation: The formation revolved like a spinning wheel, its layers constantly turning.

questions.q: Who entered the Chakravyuha alone when no one else could?
questions.explanation: Sixteen-year-old Abhimanyu pierced the wheel alone when no other Pandava warrior could.

questions.q: What crucial knowledge did the boy who entered the formation lack?
options: How to come back out of it
How to break its outer ring
How to signal for help inside
questions.explanation: Abhimanyu knew only the way in, never having learned the way out.

questions.q: Where did Abhimanyu first hear the secret of entering the Chakravyuha?
options: In a dream sent by the gods
questions.explanation: He heard Arjuna describe the entrance while still in Subhadra's womb.

questions.q: Which army formed the Chakravyuha at Kurukshetra?
questions.explanation: Drona formed the wheel with the Kaurava host while Arjuna was away.

questions.q: Which two warriors on the Pandava side could fully breach the wheel and come out again?
questions.explanation: Only Krishna and Arjuna on the Pandava side could both enter and leave the wheel.

questions.q: What was the fate of the young warrior trapped inside the wheel?
options: He was slain by many warriors together
He fought his way out at sunset
He was taken prisoner by Drona
He was rescued by Bhima's charge
questions.explanation: Six great warriors broke the rules of war to bring the boy down together.

questions.q: Why was Arjuna unable to face the Chakravyuha that day?
options: He lay wounded in the camp
He had been drawn far away by a sworn enemy band
He had vowed not to fight that day
questions.explanation: The Samsaptakas, sworn to kill Arjuna or die, drew him to a distant part of the field.

questions.q: Who led the sworn warriors who lured Arjuna away from the wheel?
questions.explanation: Susharma and his Trigarta Samsaptakas had vowed to fight Arjuna to the death.

questions.q: What was Drona's true objective in spinning the wheel formation?
options: To kill Bhima in single combat
To burn the Pandava camp by day
To force Krishna to take up arms
questions.explanation: Drona had promised Duryodhana he would take Yudhishthira captive while Arjuna was away.

questions.q: Who asked Abhimanyu to break open the Chakravyuha?
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira asked the boy to open the wheel so the army could follow him in.

questions.q: What was the Pandava plan once Abhimanyu pierced the formation's mouth?
options: He would cut his way out on the far side
He would capture Drona at the hub
Krishna would extract him by chariot
The others would pour in close behind him
questions.explanation: Bhima, Yudhishthira, and the rest were to charge in through the breach behind him.

questions.q: Who sealed the breach behind Abhimanyu, cutting him off from all help?
questions.explanation: Jayadratha of Sindhu held the breach and shut the Pandavas out.

questions.q: What divine grant allowed one king to hold back Abhimanyu's rescuers that day?
options: Shiva's boon to repel four Pandavas for a single day
Brahma's gift of an unbreakable golden armor
Indra's dart that could not miss its mark
Surya's blessing of tireless strength till dusk
questions.explanation: Shiva had granted Jayadratha the power to hold off all the Pandavas except Arjuna for one day.

questions.q: Which son of Duryodhana fell to Abhimanyu inside the wheel?
questions.explanation: Abhimanyu slew Duryodhana's son Lakshmana, enraging the Kaurava prince.

questions.q: How many maharathis finally surrounded Abhimanyu at once?
questions.explanation: Six maharathis, led by Drona and Karna, ringed the lone boy.

questions.q: Which warrior cut Abhimanyu's bow from behind?
questions.explanation: On Drona's counsel, Karna severed Abhimanyu's bow from behind.

questions.q: Deprived of his weapons, what did Abhimanyu finally take up to fight?
questions.explanation: Weaponless, Abhimanyu whirled a chariot wheel as both shield and weapon.

questions.q: Who delivered the blow that killed Abhimanyu?
questions.explanation: The son of Dushasana crushed Abhimanyu's head with a mace as both rose from the ground.

questions.q: What vow did Arjuna swear when he learned what had happened inside the wheel?
options: To slay all six attackers within three days
To kill Jayadratha by sunset next day or burn himself
To never again leave Yudhishthira's side in battle
To destroy the Trigarta host to the last man
questions.explanation: Arjuna swore to slay Jayadratha before the next sunset or enter the fire himself.

questions.q: Which list correctly names the six who brought Abhimanyu down together?
options: Drona, Karna, Kripa, Ashwatthama, Kritavarma, Brihadbala
Drona, Karna, Shalya, Duryodhana, Shakuni, Jayadratha
Karna, Kripa, Bhagadatta, Bhurishravas, Shalya, Vikarna
Drona, Ashwatthama, Duryodhana, Dushasana, Shakuni, Kritavarma
questions.explanation: Drona, Karna, Kripa, Ashwatthama, Kritavarma, and Brihadbala together overwhelmed him.

questions.q: Which king of Kosala was slain by Abhimanyu during the final encirclement?
questions.explanation: Abhimanyu killed Brihadbala, the king of Kosala, even as the six pressed in.

questions.q: In the common retelling, why was the lesson on escaping the wheel never completed in the womb?
options: Arjuna was called away to battle mid-story
Krishna deliberately cut the story short
A storm drowned out the rest of the telling
Subhadra fell asleep as Arjuna spoke
questions.explanation: The epic says only that he learned the entry; popular retellings add that Subhadra dozed off before the escape was told.

questions.q: Which agreed rule of war did Abhimanyu's death most flagrantly violate?
options: No fighting after the sun had set
Many warriors must not strike one man at once
Princes under age could not be challenged
questions.explanation: The war's code forbade many warriors from striking a single fighter at once.

questions.q: By what other name is Drona's wheel formation also known?
questions.explanation: Tradition also calls the wheel array the Padmavyuha, the lotus formation.

questions.q: Besides Krishna and Arjuna, who is sometimes said in later retellings to have known the full secret of the wheel?
questions.explanation: Some retellings say Krishna's son Pradyumna also knew the wheel's full secret.

questions.q: Which son of Shalya fell to Abhimanyu inside the formation?
questions.explanation: Abhimanyu slew Rukmaratha, the son of Shalya of Madra, within the wheel.

questions.q: Who hesitated at the formation's mouth and counseled Abhimanyu about the burden he was taking up?
options: His uncle Krishna, by a sign
The aged Drupada at his wing
questions.explanation: His charioteer Sumitra urged caution, reminding him of the burden Drona's array posed.
